title: Mnianthus ceylanicus
taxonomy: Mnianthus ceylanicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and is classified as a perennial herb. The plant belongs to the genus Mnianthus, which is a small group of flowering plants. Further classification of Mnianthus ceylanicus places it within the tribe Ajugoideae. The plant's classification is an important aspect of understanding its evolution and relationships with other species.
uses: Mnianthus ceylanicus has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in herbal remedies to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health. The plant's traditional uses highlight its importance in folk medicine, particularly in the regions where it is native.

The roots of Mnianthus ceylanicus are used to treat digestive issues and fever. Traditional medicine practitioners in Sri Lanka use the plant's parts to create remedies for various ailments.
